steps.title: Run a two-point linear regression
steps.content: The values $h(7)=21$ and $h(9)=25$ are the points $(7,21)$ and $(9,25)$. Enter $x_1=[7,9]$, $y_1=[21,25]$, and $y_1\sim mx_1+b$ in Desmos. It returns $m=2$ and $b=7$, so $h(x)=2x+7$.

steps.title: Verify with the original values
steps.content: By hand, the slope is $\frac{25-21}{9-7}=2$. Then $21=2(7)+b$, so $b=7$. Check the second value: $2(9)+7=25$, so the model fits both given points.

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
• A. $h(x) = \frac{1}{2}x - \frac{7}{2}$: slope is $\tfrac{1}{2}$, not 2; eliminated.
  • B. $h(x) = 2x + 7$: slope 2 and $h(7) = 21$, $h(9) = 25$; matches.
  • C. $h(x) = 7x + 21$: $h(7) = 70$, not 21; eliminated.
  • D. $h(x) = 9x + 25$: $h(7) = 88$, not 21; eliminated.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
